page: Microsoft Azure D16_v3 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dv3 server family. Powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor with an x86_64 architecture, this instance provides 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 8 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It features 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. The virtual machine runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor and includes 410 GB of local storage, though it does not feature any dedicated GPU hardware or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark evaluations indicate a clear division between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is relatively weak, with stress-ng, Geekbench, and single-threaded compression tests placing in the bottom 10%. However, multi-core execution scales to average levels, as demonstrated by middle-tier results in multi-core stress-ng, Geekbench, and Passmark CPU tests. Memory read bandwidth is average at up to 598.03 GB/sec for cached blocks, though large-block uncached write bandwidth is weaker, falling into the bottom 25%.

Given its balanced resource allocation, the D16_v3 is suited for general-purpose workloads that leverage multi-threaded execution rather than raw single-core speed. Appropriate applications include static web serving, software compilation, database operations, and Redis deployments, all of which demonstrate average benchmark throughput. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the hardware profile offers a stable, mid-tier resource density for legacy enterprise workloads, though it is less optimal for single-threaded tasks or modern large-scale LLM inference.
description: Microsoft Azure D16_v3 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dv3 family, configured with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 64.0 GB of RAM on an x86_64 Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor. Operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, it includes 410 GB of local storage and no GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks reveal a contrast between poor single-core execution, which ranks in the bottom 10%, and average multi-core performance across stress-ng, Geekbench, and Passmark suites. Memory read bandwidth and static web serving throughput remain in the average tier, while large-block write bandwidth is weaker. This instance is best suited for multi-threaded general-purpose workloads, including database operations, software compilation, and web serving, where resource density and balanced vCPU-to-RAM ratios are prioritized over high single-core clock speeds.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D16_v3 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 16 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 410 GB local storage, delivering average multi-core performance for database and web serving workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D16_v3 is a general-purpose VM with 16 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 410 GB local storage, optimized for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring sixteen virtual cores, local storage, and stable multi-threaded performance for standard enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor with 16 vCPUs and 64 GB RAM
Includes 410 GB of local storage for temporary data and caching
Average multi-core performance across standard CPU and database benchmarks
Poor single-core performance limits suitability for single-threaded tasks
Well-suited for static web serving, software compilation, and Redis deployments
